response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong polyglot experience, TDD discipline, and notable geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust as the primary language, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 with no Rust experience. Most recent decade is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang focused, and there is no evidence of large-scale routing pipelines, tiling, or distributed-system work at the scale this role demands.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Open Green Map projects
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems work at Sociomantic
Polyglot adaptability and willingness to work across the full stack
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience (only ~6 months at NI in 2014-2015) and no Rust
No demonstrated experience with routing tiles, map data pipelines, or navigation guidance systems
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms, not systems-level Linux server engineering
